prompt_config.task_instruction: Analyze the following employee recognition messages and build a taxonomy of recognition categories. Use the provided seed categories as starting points, but freely add new categories or discard seeds that aren't supported by the data.
prompt_config.additional_constraints: Keep 5-8 top-level categories. Merge or split seeds as the data warrants.
prompt_config.full_composed_prompt:
You are a researcher designing a taxonomy for employee recognition awards.
Below are 100 real award messages from a company's recognition platform.

MESSAGES:
Message 1:
  Award: Launch-Ready Legend
  From: Web Developer III → To: Developer III
  Geoff, you absolutely crushed the ABM page while I was away. From building brand-new components to setting up the full experience, you had everything prepped and polished for launch the second I got back. Thank you for stepping up and keeping momentum strong—you made the handoff seamless.

---

This achievement is significant as it will serve as the foundation for the Spend 2.0 initiatives we will focus on in 2026. Your dedication, forward-thinking approach, and commitment to quality are inspirational. You have driven this work ahead on your own, while the team’s primary focus was on backend development to meet cus

INSTRUCTIONS:
Analyze the following employee recognition messages and build a taxonomy of recognition categories. Use the provided seed categories as starting points, but freely add new categories or discard seeds that aren't supported by the data.

Consider these starting categories as hints, but freely add new ones or discard these if the data doesn't support them:
- Leadership & Management
- Innovation & Business Impact
- Customer & Client Focus
- Teamwork & Collaboration
- Professional Excellence
- Social Responsibility & Culture

Additional guidance:
Keep 5-8 top-level categories. Merge or split seeds as the data warrants.

Output ONLY valid JSON with this structure:
{
  "categories": [
    {
      "id": "A",
      "name": "...",
      "description": "...",
      "subcategories": [
        {
          "id": "A1",
          "name": "...",
          "description": "...",
          "examples": ["short phrase from messages", "another phrase"]
        }
      ]
    }
  ],
  "reasoning": "2-3 sentences explaining why this structure fits the data"
}

categories.name: Teamwork & Collaboration
categories.description: Recognition for working together, supporting each other, and achieving common goals

subcategories.name: Support and Assistance
subcategories.description: Helping colleagues with tasks, projects, or difficulties

subcategories.name: Partnership and Cooperation
subcategories.description: Collaborating with others to achieve shared objectives

categories.name: Professional Excellence
categories.description: Recognition for outstanding performance, skills, and dedication

subcategories.name: Exceptional Performance
subcategories.description: Achieving exceptional results, meeting challenges, and exceeding expectations

subcategories.name: Expertise and Knowledge
subcategories.description: Demonstrating specialized skills, expertise, and knowledge

categories.name: Customer & Client Focus
categories.description: Recognition for delivering exceptional customer service and support

subcategories.name: Customer Satisfaction
subcategories.description: Providing excellent service, resolving issues, and meeting customer needs

categories.name: Innovation & Business Impact
categories.description: Recognition for innovative ideas, initiatives, and contributions to business success

subcategories.name: Innovative Solutions
subcategories.description: Developing and implementing new ideas, products, or processes

subcategories.name: Business Results
subcategories.description: Achieving significant business outcomes, such as revenue growth or cost savings
examples: thanks for your contribution to our success

categories.name: Leadership & Management
categories.description: Recognition for effective leadership, guidance, and mentorship

subcategories.name: Leadership and Guidance
subcategories.description: Providing direction, support, and mentorship to teams or individuals

categories.name: Social Responsibility & Culture
categories.description: Recognition for promoting a positive work culture, social responsibility, and community involvement

subcategories.name: Community Involvement
subcategories.description: Participating in or organizing community events, charity work, or volunteer activities

subcategories.name: Positive Work Culture
subcategories.description: Fostering a positive, inclusive, and supportive work environment
examples: thanks for creating a positive work culture
appreciate your contribution to our team's morale

taxonomy.reasoning: This structure fits the data because it groups similar recognition themes together, such as teamwork, professional excellence, and customer focus. The subcategories provide more specific examples of each theme, allowing for a more nuanced understanding of the recognition messages. The six top-level categories provide a clear and concise framework for organizing the data, making it easier to analyze and understand the types of recognition being given.
